from __future__ import annotations

import unittest

from src.trader.execution.allocation_phase3_suite_runner import (
    ALLOCATION_PHASE3_PATCH_RANGE,
    ALLOCATION_PHASE3_TEST_MODULES,
    AllocationPhase3SuiteRunnerError,
    build_allocation_phase3_suite_args,
    build_allocation_phase3_suite_command,
    build_allocation_phase3_suite_definition,
    build_allocation_phase3_suite_metadata,
    validate_allocation_phase3_suite,
)


class TestAllocationPhase3SuiteRunner(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_definition_matches_expected_patch_range(self) -> None:
        definition = build_allocation_phase3_suite_definition()

        self.assertEqual(definition.suite_name, "allocation_phase3_suite")
        self.assertEqual(
            (definition.patch_range_start, definition.patch_range_end),
            ALLOCATION_PHASE3_PATCH_RANGE,
        )
        self.assertEqual(definition.test_modules, ALLOCATION_PHASE3_TEST_MODULES)

    def test_validation_returns_unique_ordered_modules(self) -> None:
        modules = validate_allocation_phase3_suite()
        self.assertEqual(modules, ALLOCATION_PHASE3_TEST_MODULES)
        self.assertEqual(len(modules), len(set(modules)))

    def test_suite_args_include_all_phase3_modules(self) -> None:
        args = build_allocation_phase3_suite_args()
        self.assertEqual(args[:3], ["-m", "unittest", "-v"])
        self.assertEqual(tuple(args[3:]), ALLOCATION_PHASE3_TEST_MODULES)

    def test_command_builder_supports_custom_python_program(self) -> None:
        command = build_allocation_phase3_suite_command("py")
        self.assertEqual(command[0], "py")
        self.assertEqual(command[1:], build_allocation_phase3_suite_args())

    def test_metadata_surfaces_command_and_counts(self) -> None:
        metadata = build_allocation_phase3_suite_metadata()
        self.assertEqual(metadata["validated_module_count"], len(ALLOCATION_PHASE3_TEST_MODULES))
        self.assertEqual(metadata["command_args"], build_allocation_phase3_suite_args())

    def test_duplicate_modules_fail_closed(self) -> None:
        with self.assertRaises(AllocationPhase3SuiteRunnerError):
            validate_allocation_phase3_suite(
                ["tests.test_allocation_candidate", "tests.test_allocation_candidate"]
            )

    def test_empty_module_name_fails_closed(self) -> None:
        with self.assertRaises(AllocationPhase3SuiteRunnerError):
            validate_allocation_phase3_suite(["tests.test_allocation_candidate", "  "])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

# Allocation Phase 3 Suite Runner

## Purpose

Patch 171 introduces the named allocator Phase 3 suite runner.

Patches 164 through 170 established the allocator Phase 3 building blocks:
- chain and strategy budget policy,
- allocation candidates,
- allocation decisions,
- append-only decision storage,
- final portfolio constraint gating,
- review packet rendering,
- replay audit.

Patch 171 creates one canonical suite definition that groups those test surfaces together.

## Why this layer exists

The allocator Phase 3 work is now large enough that operator and handoff flows need one named proof surface instead of remembering many separate test module names.

This patch gives the project:

- one canonical suite name,
- one canonical patch range,
- one canonical ordered module list,
- one canonical unittest command shape.

## Core responsibilities

This layer must:

- define the allocator Phase 3 test module set,
- fail closed if the suite is empty or malformed,
- preserve deterministic ordering,
- expose command args for runner scripts and PatchOps manifests,
- keep the suite logic in trader execution code, not in ad hoc shell glue.

## What this layer does not do

This patch does not:

- change allocator behavior,
- widen live behavior,
- replace the earlier narrow tests,
- move business logic into PatchOps.

It only defines and proves the named test suite surface.

## Interaction with nearby patches

- Patch 164 introduced chain strategy budget policy.
- Patch 165 introduced allocation candidates.
- Patch 166 introduced allocation decisions.
- Patch 167 introduced append-only decision storage.
- Patch 168 introduced the final portfolio constraint gate.
- Patch 169 introduced review packet rendering.
- Patch 170 introduced replay audit.
- Patch 171 groups those surfaces into one canonical Phase 3 suite.

## Validation surface

The patch should prove at least these behaviors:

- suite definition exposes the expected patch range,
- suite validation preserves ordered unique modules,
- command args include all allocator Phase 3 test modules,
- custom python launcher names are supported,
- malformed suite definitions fail closed.

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

$projectRoot = "C:\dev\trader"
$venvPython = Join-Path $projectRoot ".venv\Scripts\python.exe"

if (-not (Test-Path $venvPython)) {
    throw "Expected venv python not found: $venvPython"
}

& $venvPython -m unittest -v `
    tests.test_allocation_phase3_suite_runner `
    tests.test_chain_strategy_budget_policy `
    tests.test_allocation_candidate `
    tests.test_allocation_decision `
    tests.test_allocation_decision_store `
    tests.test_final_portfolio_constraint_gate `
    tests.test_allocation_review_packet_builder `
    tests.test_allocation_replay_report

notes: Patch 171 all-in-one PatchOps runner with inline authored content.
